frame_analyses.description: Title screen showing 'Laparoscopic Extracorporeal Suturing & Knot Tying'
Penrose drain visible with two marked target points, needle driver approaching
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clear visualization of target marks on orange Penrose drain

frame_analyses.description: Needle driver grasping suture needle, positioning for entry through first mark
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appropriate needle grasp position, good approach angle

frame_analyses.description: Suture placed through drain with both ends visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture appears to pass through both marked points accurately

frame_analyses.description: Hands visible outside trainer forming extracorporeal knot
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper extracorporeal technique with hands outside the trainer

frame_analyses.description: Knot pusher being used to advance completed knot down to tissue
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appropriate use of knot pusher instrument

frame_analyses.description: Knot being positioned and tightened at drain surface
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good knot positioning technique, appears to be seating properly

frame_analyses.description: Scissors being used to cut suture tails
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appropriate cutting technique with both tails visible

frame_analyses.description: Final view of completed suture with knot in place
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot appears secure and appropriately positioned

penalties.description: Entry point appears approximately 1mm from first mark

confidence_rationale: Clear visualization of most key elements including knot formation and placement. Some uncertainty in exact measurement of suture deviation from marks due to camera angle and resolution.
cannot_determine: Exact millimeter measurements of suture deviation due to camera angle limitations
Whether the knot is a proper surgeon's knot vs simple knot due to extracorporeal formation
technique_summary: Excellent expert-level performance with efficient suture placement and proper extracorporeal knot technique. Completion time of 78 seconds falls within expert range (60-100s).
improvement_suggestions: Focus on more precise needle entry exactly at the marked points
Consider slightly faster knot pushing phase
